reasoning: The question asks whether DXCM will close higher than its Aug 15 close (80.95) on four future dates. The time-series analysis provides a combined estimate of P(> 80.95) = 0.42 based on a downward medium-term linear trend over the last 3 months, despite some recent upward momentum. As instructed, I use this 0.42 as my primary anchor for the shortest resolution date (1 week ahead: Aug 24). DXCM recently suffered a post-earnings drop in late July 2025 to ~75 and quickly rebounded to 80.95, increasing the likelihood of some near-term mean-reversion downward. Over longer horizons, the predictive power of current local momentum and specific seasonal signals naturally decays. Assuming stock prices basically follow a random walk with drift, the probabilities of being above the current price generally trend back to, and slightly above, 0.50 due to the equity market risk premium. Accordingly, the probability smoothly drifts from the 0.42 anchor back toward 0.50 for the longest horizon (February 2026).
